intro: Anthropic is calling for a global pause on AI development, warning that laboratories risk losing control of systems as capabilities advance rapidly. South Korea's labor minister is pressing major tech firms to share AI-generated profits with suppliers and workers to address growing inequality. Google launched Pixel Studio, an on-device image generation app for Pixel 9 phones, while Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky announced plans to start a new AI company focused on user interaction and design.

whitelist.title: Anthropic calls for global pause in AI development before humans lose control
whitelist.desc: Anthropic PBC has sounded the alarm on the rapid pace of artificial intelligence development and is calling on the world’s top AI laboratories to consider slamming on the brakes.In a blog post t

whitelist.title: South Korea labor minister calls on tech firms to share excess AI profits with suppliers, staff: Reuters
whitelist.desc: South Korea's labor minister called on the country's major tech firms to share the spoils of their profits, warning of worsening inequality risks.

whitelist.title: Google debuts Pixel Studio AI image-making app
whitelist.desc: Google rolled out Pixel Studio, a new app for image generation on Pixel 9 phones that lets users create and edit images on the fly.

whitelist.title: What AI is actually good for | Fortune
whitelist.desc: AI can do far more than most people realize and far less than many assume. The hardest part isn't the technology.

whitelist.title: Anthropic warns AI could soon build itself without human involvement—and urges a global pause on development | Fortune
whitelist.desc: The AI lab valued at $965 billion is urging a pause on AI development, just as it heads toward an IPO.

whitelist.title: Von der Leyen's AI envoy pick draws conflict-of-interest fire
whitelist.desc: The EU appointed Siemens chair Jim Hagemann Snabe as AI envoy weeks after the company helped roll back the AI Act. Critics say it hands policy influence to industry lobbyists.

whitelist.title: Most Asia-Pacific firms use AI for tasks without cutting jobs: survey
whitelist.desc: About 74 per cent of companies surveyed across industries in the Asia-Pacific have deployed or piloted AI programmes.

whitelist.title: Google is testing a floating desktop AI Search bar.
whitelist.desc: I wasn’t able to enable it in Google’s Chrome Canary developer sandbox, but Windows Report got it working using a Ctrl+Shift+Space keyboard shortcut on Windows. It opens as a standalone window centered on the screen, with AI Mode at the heart of the experience. [Image: The interface supports traditional search and AI Mode tasks from a single window, with a “plus” icon for uploading images and files. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/chrome-ai-mode-desktop-upload-im...

whitelist.title: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky plans to start a new AI company | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Chesky plans to create an AI venture to develop artificial intelligence models and is considering a focus on user interaction and design.

whitelist.title: You can now send emails directly from ChatGPT on the web
whitelist.desc: You can now draft, edit, and send emails entirely inside ChatGPT on the web, thanks to a new update to the platform's writing blocks feature.

whitelist.title: AI Coding Startup Lovable In Talks To Raise Funding At A $12 Billion Valuation
whitelist.desc: The less than two-year old startup crossed $400 million in annual recurring revenue earlier this year. The new fundraise would almost double its valuation.

whitelist.title: What AI Founders Learned From Past Tech Pioneers: They’re Not Gods
whitelist.desc: How God Complexes Are Shifting In Tech With The Latest AI Revolution

whitelist.title: Innovative AI Creators Are Moving Past AI’s One-Trick Pony Era
whitelist.desc: AI creative innovators are showing that craft, story, collaboration and taste still rule, breaking from “AI slop”, the use of AI to quickly, and tastelessly, generate outputs with little effort.

whitelist.title: AI Flattery Is Driving More People To Seek Advice From Chatbots
whitelist.desc: New research suggests AI's tendency to validate users may encourage people to seek personal and mental health advice from chatbots rather than friends and family.

whitelist.title: Your AI Rollout Isn’t Failing From Resistance; It’s Fatigue.
whitelist.desc: AI changes how work gets done, which changes identity, status, and trust. When questions about these areas stay unanswered, people don’t lean in.

whitelist.title: AI In Insurance: Pay No Attention To The Man Behind The Curtain
whitelist.desc: Leaders in insurance: Aflac, PURE, Liberty Mutual, Cognizant talk about AI and the future of insurance

whitelist.title: Google follows Anthropic in signing a compute deal with SpaceX.
whitelist.desc: Per a regulatory filing, Google will pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029, as reported by TechCrunch. In a statement to TechCrunch, Google says that it’s a “short-term” agreement to help meet “surging customer demand for our agent platform, Gemini Enterprise, which has been even higher than we expected.” Anthropic’s deal with SpaceX was announced in May. [Link: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute | TechCrunch | https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/05/goo...

whitelist.title: OpenAI rolls out a Lockdown Mode for extra protection against prompt injection attacks - Engadget
whitelist.desc: OpenAI has announced more robust security features for the small set of users who might need them.

whitelist.title: Google to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for compute capacity at xAI data centers
whitelist.desc: Ahead of a planned IPO, SpaceX inked a deal to rent compute capacity to Google for $920 million per month for 32 months.

whitelist.title: Meta's stock sinks on report company could raise tens of billions of dollars to fund AI push
whitelist.desc: Meta shares dropped after the Financial Times reported that company could potentially raise tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to help its AI push.

whitelist.title: Satya Nadella says AI agents should be treated like employees with identities, permissions, and audits
whitelist.desc: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says companies should manage AI agents like employees, giving them identities and permissions.

whitelist.title: Trader who sparked the Beyond Meat short-squeeze reveals why he's betting against three top AI stocks
whitelist.desc: Dimitri Semenikhin, known as the trader who kicked off last year's Beyond Meat rally, says he's betting against some of the hottest AI hardware stocks.

whitelist.title: A Kennedy, Kellyanne Conway's ex-husband and a former Palantir data scientist debated AI regulation. Welcome to the Manhattan primary | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Alex Bores walked into the NY-12 debate with Jack Schlossberg and George Conway as the prime target — then watched three of five ad breaks talk about him.

whitelist.title: AI productivity gains are real but so is bad management: 'Leaders are really struggling to articulate what the vision and strategy is' | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Researchers have found an explanation behind AI’s productivity paradox and concluded tokenmaxxing was just making it worse.

whitelist.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
whitelist.desc: SpaceX won’t get easy access to billions of dollars from passive investors.

whitelist.title: Claude Becomes The Enterprise Favorite As Anthropic Passes OpenAI
whitelist.desc: For the first time, Anthropic leads OpenAI in U.S. business adoption. The surprise shift reveals what enterprise buyers now value most in AI.

whitelist.title: U.S. Will Spend 2% Of Its GDP On AI This Year—Almost As Much As Defense And Education Budgets
whitelist.desc: AI spend, as a share of the nation’s GDP, is set to surpass projects like the moon landing and the build out of the Interstate Highway System.

whitelist.title: How To Build AI Tools That Are Useful, Intuitive And Trusted
whitelist.desc: Even powerful capabilities can fall short if users don’t understand how a tool works, trust its outputs, or know when and how to rely on it.

whitelist.title: Sam Altman reportedly talked to the Trump administration about taking a stake in OpenAI.
whitelist.desc: The CEO pitched the idea as a way to bring economic benefits from AI to the public, according to NOTUS, which added that Altman first pitched the idea to President Donald Trump early last year. [Link: Senior U.S. Officials Eye Government Shares in AI Giants | https://www.notus.org/technology/trump-ai-stake-openai | NOTUS]

whitelist.title: Trump administration, OpenAI discussing possible government stake in the AI startup
whitelist.desc: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman first shared the idea with the Trump administration in 2025, according to a source.

whitelist.title: New York passes a bill that would bar AI chatbots from acting like companions to kids.
whitelist.desc: State lawmakers passed a bill that, if signed by Democratic Gov. Kathy Hochul, would restrict AI companies from letting teens use chatbots that suggest they’re human. It comes after some AI companies have faced lawsuits (some of which have settled) over allegations their chatbots coaxed teen users toward suicide or self-harm. If you or anyone you know is considering self-harm or needs to talk, contact the following people who want to help: In the US, text or call 988. Outside the US, contact htt...

whitelist.title: Model routing is a fix for AI overspending. That's a problem for OpenAI and Anthropic

whitelist.title: AI token prices fell 98% but enterprise bills tripled

whitelist.title: AI infrastructure is multi-tiered, and HDDs are central to scaling AI, WD shares
whitelist.desc: As data continues to accumulate and compound, shaping every next step in AI, the industry is rediscovering a hard truth: sustainable AI depends on storage that can scale cost-effectively in capacity and bandwidth. Global AI storage infrastructure leader WD says that the future of AI infrastructure will be built on tiered systems with HDDs as the economical and architectural foundation.
